name: Sant Martí Sacalm and Susqueda Reservoir Loop
name_original: Sant Martí Sacalm & el Coll - Circular desde el valle del Llémana
description: This difficult 57.0-mile road cycling route offers challenging climbs, rural scenery, and great views from Sant Martí Sacalm.
tips: In this beautiful valley you will always come across small, idyllic farms and settlements that have settled on the banks of the Llémena river. Wonderful rural ambience and moderate incline - perfect for racing cycling. ;)

The climb to Sant Marti Sacalm, with its eight kilometers in length and a gradient of around seven percent, is rather average. Nevertheless, it is definitely a recommendation. Because it is very scenic and the quiet, completely paved road can be driven perfectly. Due to its compactness, you can go full throttle from bottom to top and reward yourself with a great view and a short coffee stop in the small café.
Amazing road both ways, no cars and beautiful views all around the lake!
The road up there ends in gravel. So it's up and down again. But totally worth it.

faqs.question: Is the Sant Marti Sacalm & el Coll circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is considered a difficult road ride, requiring very good fitness. It spans over 91 kilometers with nearly 2,000 meters of elevation gain, including the challenging Sant Marti Sacalm climb. It's best suited for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly paved, with approximately 85.6 km of asphalt and 5.94 km of other paved surfaces. While the climb to Sant Martí Sacalm is described as completely paved, some segments of the circular route may be unpaved and difficult to ride, potentially requiring you to push your bike. The Llémena Valley section features smooth roads.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sant Marti Sacalm & el Coll circular route?
faqs.answer: This challenging route typically takes around 5 hours and 10 minutes of active cycling time to complete. However, factoring in stops for rest, refreshments, and enjoying the views, you should plan for a longer overall duration.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience beautiful scenery and a wonderful rural ambiance throughout. Key highlights include the idyllic Llémena Valley with its small farms, the very scenic climb to Sant Martí Sacalm offering great views from the top, and glimpses of the Susqueda Reservoir.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sant Marti Sacalm & el Coll circular route?
faqs.answer: While the starting point is in the Llemana Valley, specific parking information isn't detailed. It's advisable to look for public parking options in one of the small towns or villages within the Llémena Valley, ensuring it's safe and doesn't obstruct local residents.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, especially on climbs, and winters might bring colder temperatures or less predictable weather, though the region generally has a mild climate.

faqs.question: Is the Sant Marti Sacalm & el Coll circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in the surrounding natural areas, bringing a dog on a long, difficult road cycling trip like this is not practical or recommended for their safety or comfort.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or special regulations for cycling in Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona?
faqs.answer: As this route passes through the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona, it's important to respect natural park regulations. Generally, no specific permits are required for cycling on established roads and trails, but always stay on marked paths, do not disturb wildlife, and carry out all your waste. Check the official park website for any specific seasonal restrictions or updated guidelines.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are a couple of options. At the summit of Sant Martí Sacalm, there's a basic cafe where you can stop for coffee. Additionally, the route passes by Hostal del Coll, which is a restaurant where you can refuel.

faqs.question: Why is this route popular with professional cyclists?
faqs.answer: The area, particularly the Sant Martí Sacalm climb, is a well-known training ground for professional cyclists, especially those based in Girona. The climb itself is often used as a 20-minute power test due to its consistent 8-kilometer length and average 7% gradient, offering a quiet and scenic challenge.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Notable ones include sections of the Via cicloturística InterCatalunya, BTT la Selva - Circular Nord, Camí de Sant Jaume (Sant Pere de Rodes - Montserrat), and Ruta del Ter.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for tackling the Sant Marti Sacalm climb?
faqs.answer: The climb to Sant Martí Sacalm is a consistent ascent without much leveling off, making it a steady effort. Pace yourself from the start, as it's about 8 kilometers long with an average gradient of 7%. Enjoy the quiet, well-paved road and the rewarding views from the top.
